title: Literature Survey: KL-Constrained EM, Misspecified HMMs, and Robust Markov Optimization
summary: Comprehensive literature survey integrating three research domains critical for robust HMM learning under order misspecification: (1) KL-constrained and distributionally robust EM algorithms, (2) convergence theory under HMM model misspecification, and (3) KL-constrained optimization for Markov chains. Synthesizes 18 key references with identification of five critical research gaps.
answer:
This literature survey integrates three previously disconnected research areas to support robust parameter learning for HMMs under order mismatch.

**Area 1: KL-Constrained and Robust EM [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]** provides the foundational framework. Duchi et al. [1, 2] establish distributionally robust optimization with KL divergence, developing convex formulations and finite-sample minimax bounds showing robustness sometimes incurs convergence cost [1]. Kunstner et al. [3] prove that EM for exponential families is equivalent to mirror descent, achieving convergence in KL divergence with non-asymptotic rates and local superlinear convergence [3]. The connection to Bregman divergences [4, 5] enables incorporating convex constraints through projections while preserving multiplicative structure and convergence guarantees [4, 5].

**Area 2: HMM Misspecification [6, 7, 8, 9]** characterizes what happens when the true model order differs from the fitted order. Hsu, Kakade, and Zhang [6] provide finite-sample spectral learning with sample complexity independent of observation space size, requiring only separation conditions on HMM spectral properties [6]. Douc and Moulines [7] prove that the MLE remains consistent under misspecification, converging to the KL-divergence minimizer (pseudo-true parameter) [7]. Critically, Dwivedi et al. [8] show that when the Fisher Information Matrix becomes singular under misspecification (as occurs with order mismatch), convergence rate deteriorates from O(1/√n) to dimension-dependent rates like O((d/n)^(1/4)) [8]. Alexandrovich et al. [9] characterize identifiability conditions for HMM structure, requiring full-rank transition matrices and distinct emission distributions [9].

**Area 3: Markov Chain and Constrained Optimization [10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15]** provides geometric tools. The Kullback-Leibler divergence rate extends KL divergence to sequences [10], and information projection (I-projection) minimizes KL subject to linear constraints on exponential families [11, 13]. Csiszár's geometry [13, 14] establishes a Pythagorean theorem for KL divergence on exponential families (dually flat manifolds), enabling projection-based algorithms [13, 14]. Rate-distortion theory [15] connects model reduction of Markov chains to KL divergence minimization, characterizing the cost of aggregating state spaces [15].

**Convergence Theory Foundations [16]** provide baseline results. Balakrishnan et al. [16] establish population and finite-sample convergence guarantees for EM in well-specified settings, characterizing geometric convergence to KL projection [16]. This baseline is essential for understanding how order mismatch degrades convergence compared to well-specified EM [16].

**Algorithmic Foundations [17, 18]** ground the proposed work. The Baum-Welch algorithm [17] is the foundational algorithm for HMM parameter estimation via forward-backward probabilities with guaranteed monotone log-likelihood increase [17]. Constrained EM variants [18] demonstrate practical application of KL-constrained optimization in dynamic systems with policy bounds [18].

**Critical Gaps Identified:** [1] No work applies KL-constrained EM to forward-backward algorithms (latent-variable HMMs with constraints on transition and emission parameters). [2] Finite-sample convergence rates for robust EM under order mismatch are not characterized—it is unknown whether the O((d/n)^(1/4)) singularity rate from Dwivedi et al. [8] applies when using KL-constrained updates. [3] The relationship between KL divergence bias (between true k-th order and best first-order model) and robust parameter recovery remains unquantified. [4] No algorithmic method exists to compute or estimate the KL divergence KL(P_true_k || P_best_first_order) or to set constraint radius ε adaptively based on order mismatch. [5] Viterbi decoding error under constrained parameters has not been analyzed, leaving robust inference under order mismatch unresolved.

sources.title: Learning Models with Uniform Performance via Distributionally Robust Optimization
sources.summary: Develops DRO framework for robust model learning with KL-divergence constraints; proves finite-sample minimax bounds showing robustness sometimes incurs convergence cost.

sources.title: Statistics of Robust Optimization: A Generalized Empirical Likelihood Approach
sources.summary: Develops generalized empirical likelihood framework for f-divergence balls; analyzes statistical inference and confidence intervals under distributional uncertainty.

sources.title: Homeomorphic-Invariance of EM: Non-Asymptotic Convergence in KL Divergence for Exponential Families via Mirror Descent
sources.venue: Proceedings of Machine Learning Research, Vol. 130
sources.summary: Proves EM for exponential families is mirror descent with KL divergence regularizer; establishes non-asymptotic convergence rates in KL divergence with local superlinear convergence.

sources.title: Reinterpreting EMML as Mirror Descent for Constrained Maximum Likelihood Estimation
sources.summary: Shows EMML equivalence to mirror descent; demonstrates how to incorporate convex constraints through Bregman projections while preserving convergence structure.

sources.title: Bregman Divergence and Mirror Descent
sources.summary: Comprehensive treatment of Bregman divergences in optimization; shows mirror descent replaces Euclidean distance with Bregman divergence, enabling constrained optimization.

sources.title: A Spectral Algorithm for Learning Hidden Markov Models
sources.venue: Journal of Computer and System Sciences, Vol. 78, No. 5
sources.summary: Develops polynomial-time spectral algorithm for HMM learning with finite-sample guarantees; sample complexity independent of observation space size.

sources.title: Asymptotic properties of the maximum likelihood estimation in misspecified hidden Markov models
sources.summary: Proves consistency of MLE when true model is not in parametric family; characterizes convergence to KL-divergence minimizer under misspecification.

sources.title: Singularity, Misspecification, and the Convergence Rate of EM
sources.summary: Characterizes non-standard convergence rates for EM under model misspecification; shows O((d/n)^(1/4)) rate when Fisher Information becomes singular.

sources.title: Nonparametric Identification and Maximum Likelihood Estimation for Hidden Markov Models
sources.summary: Establishes identifiability conditions for HMM parameters: full-rank transition matrix, ergodicity, and distinct emission distributions required for parameter recovery.

sources.title: Notes on the KL-divergence between a Markov chain and product space interpretation
sources.summary: Analyzes KL divergence between Markov chains, showing how marginal distributions relate under aggregation and projection operations.

sources.title: Optimal Kullback-Leibler Aggregation via Information Projection
sources.summary: Develops algorithms for KL-constrained aggregation on exponential families using information projections with semi-closed-form solutions.

sources.title: A rate-distortion framework for MCMC algorithms: geometry and factorization of multivariate Markov chains
sources.summary: Analyzes rate-distortion theory for Markov chains; connects to KL divergence rates and optimal model reduction geometry.

sources.title: Information Projections Revisited
sources.summary: Establishes Pythagorean theorem for KL divergence in exponential families; shows I-projections minimize KL divergence onto constraint submanifolds.

sources.title: Lecture 7: Information Projections and Exponential Families
sources.summary: Comprehensive treatment of information projections on exponential families and the role of Bregman divergences in constrained optimization algorithms.

sources.title: Rate-Distortion via Markov Chain Monte Carlo
sources.venue: IEEE Workshop on Statistical Signal Processing
sources.summary: Rate-distortion framework for Markov chain processes; characterizes compression cost via KL divergence rates for model reduction.

sources.title: Statistical guarantees for the EM algorithm: From population to sample
sources.summary: Establishes population and finite-sample convergence guarantees for EM in well-specified settings; characterizes geometric convergence to KL projection.

sources.title: Baum-Welch Algorithm
sources.summary: Foundational algorithm for HMM parameter estimation via forward-backward probabilities; guaranteed to monotonically increase log-likelihood.

sources.title: Constrained Expectation-Maximization Methods for Effective Reinforcement Learning
sources.summary: Develops constrained EM variants with KL divergence policy constraints; demonstrates practical applications in dynamic systems.

follow_up_questions: What is the explicit closed-form solution (or iterative algorithm) for the KL-constrained M-step when parameterizing transition and emission matrices as exponential families? Can it be computed via Bregman projections?
How does the constraint radius ε scale with order mismatch magnitude (k_true - k_fit)? Is there an information-theoretic lower bound on ε to preserve identifiability?

What is the finite-sample convergence rate for constrained Baum-Welch under order mismatch? Does the O((d/n)^(1/4)) singularity rate from Dwivedi et al. apply when using KL-constrained updates instead of unconstrained EM?
Can the KL divergence KL(P_true_k || P_best_first_order) be estimated adaptively from data, and does this enable setting ε automatically based on observed divergence?
How does the Viterbi algorithm's decoding error scale under constrained parameters with order mismatch? What is the relationship between parameter constraint radius and state inference error?
How do the spectral learning guarantees from Hsu et al. extend to KL-constrained settings? Are separation conditions preserved or weakened?
What are the convergence guarantees for forward-backward algorithms under constraints? Does the E-step still have closed form when parameters are constrained to a KL ball?
